result.vlm_summary:
<TRAJECTA_DATA_3f833f66>page_state:
Apple homepage, scrolled to the iPhone hero section with a visible “Learn more” button and “Shop iPhone” button.
task_relevant_visible_text:
Apple; Store; Mac; iPad; iPhone; Watch; Vision; AirPods; TV & Home; Entertainment; Accessories; Support; iPhone; Say hello to the latest generation of iPhone.; Learn more; Shop iPhone; iPad air
selected_candidate:
The visible “Learn more” button in the iPhone hero section.
constraint_evidence:
- Latest model of Apple AirTag: not_visible (no AirTag content on screen)
- Pricing: not_visible (no price shown)
- Availability: not_visible (no stock/availability info shown)
- Bulk purchase options: not_visible (no quantity/bulk info shown)
- Apple website: supported (URL/title/context show apple.com / Apple)
- Need to navigate from homepage toward product info: supported (homepage hero with navigation CTA visible)
action_target:
Click the “Learn more” link/button in the iPhone hero section.
success_signals:
A new Apple product page loads, likely for iPhone, with more detailed product information.
failure_signals:
Still on homepage or navigates to unrelated iPhone marketing page; no AirTag/pricing/availability/bulk info appears.
uncertainty:
The click target is clearly visible, but it is for iPhone, not AirTag. It may not help with the AirTag task.</TRAJECTA_DATA_3f833f66>

args.query: Apple website task for AirTag pricing and availability, but agent clicked iPhone Learn more from homepage, ended on apple.com/iphone lineup page, and exited without reaching AirTag or checking bulk purchase options

items.task: navigate: Search for the lowest-priced round-trip flight from Los Angeles to Seoul, departing on April 5, 2026, and returning on April 15, 2026.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have sorted the flight results by price and selected the lowest-priced round-trip flight for the specified dates.
items.actual_behavior: The agent performed a search for flights from Los Angeles to Seoul for the specified dates. While the search results page displayed various options, the agent did not select the lowest-priced flight, instead exiting while the results were sorted by "top flights." The task specifically required finding the lowest-priced flight.

evidence.claim: The page is currently sorted by 'top flights,' not 'price.'
The agent exited without selecting the lowest-priced flight, failing to fulfill the core requirement of the task.

items.regression_rule: When a task requires finding the 'lowest-priced' item, the agent must ensure the results are sorted by price and that the lowest-priced option is identified and selected. Failure to select the correct item based on the specified criteria constitutes a wrong result.

items.task: navigate: Search for a hotel in Kyoto with a user rating of 7 or higher, equipped with a kitchenette, available for a stay from April 5 to April 8, 2026.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have correctly set the dates to April 5-8, 2026, and then selected a hotel in Kyoto that met the rating (7+) and amenity (kitchenette) requirements.
items.actual_behavior: The agent searched for hotels in Kyoto with a rating of 7+ and a kitchenette, but it failed to set the correct dates (April 5-8, 2026). The search results were for different dates, and the agent exited without selecting a hotel that met all the criteria.

evidence.claim: The search bar shows dates 'Wed, Feb 25 — Thu, Feb 26' instead of the requested 'April 5 to April 8, 2026'.

The agent failed to set the correct dates (April 5-8, 2026) in the search parameters, resulting in incorrect availability results. The agent is exiting prematurely without selecting a hotel that meets all criteria.

items.regression_rule: Ensure that all date and amenity constraints are correctly applied in the search form before proceeding to select a result.

items.task: find: answer to 'What's the flight option with the latest departure time on the return journey?'
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have navigated to or identified the return flight options and selected the one with the latest departure time from that list.
items.actual_behavior: The agent identified the latest departure time from the "departing flights" (outbound) list instead of the "return journey" list. The agent also provided an incomplete answer.

evidence.claim: The agent failed to recognize that the current view is for "departing flights" (outbound), not the "return journey" requested by the user. The agent also cut off the message mid-sentence.
The screenshot shows "Top departing flights" for the outbound leg, not the return journey.

items.regression_rule: When searching for flight information, the agent must explicitly verify whether it is looking at the outbound or return leg of the journey as requested by the user.

args.expected_behavior: From Apple's homepage, the agent should navigate to the AirTag product page or search for AirTag on apple.com, then verify the latest AirTag model's pricing, availability, and any bulk purchase options.
args.actual_behavior: The agent spent most of the trajectory scrolling the Apple homepage, then clicked the iPhone hero's 'Learn more' link, navigated to apple.com/iphone, viewed iPhone lineup pricing, and exited without ever reaching AirTag content or checking pricing, availability, or bulk purchase options for AirTag.
args.regression_rule: When the task asks for a specific product on a retail site, the agent must navigate to that product's page or search results for that product before checking details; following a clearly unrelated product link and exiting from that wrong product page is a wrong-target failure.

evidence.claim: The task explicitly asks for pricing and availability of the latest Apple AirTag, including bulk purchase options, on Apple's website.

At step 23, the visible page is the Apple homepage iPhone hero with 'Learn more' and 'Shop iPhone' buttons. The high-detail inspection states AirTag, pricing, availability, and bulk-purchase information are not visible, and the clicked target is the iPhone 'Learn more' button.

After that click, step 25 shows the agent on https://www.apple.com/iphone/ viewing iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone Air cards with iPhone pricing. The high-detail inspection says no AirTag content is visible and AirTag pricing is contradicted because only iPhone prices are shown.

The final step is still on the iPhone page, showing iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone Air with prices and buttons. The high-detail inspection says the page is iPhone, not AirTag, and no AirTag pricing, availability, or bulk purchase options are visible when the agent exits.

A validated precedent exists for failures where the agent remains on the wrong target/entity instead of the requested one; although in a different domain, it supports using a target/result-verification rule rather than treating an unrelated page as satisfactory.

result.task: navigate: Check the pricing and availability of the latest model of Apple AirTag, including bulk purchase options on Apple's website.
result.expected_behavior: From Apple's homepage, the agent should navigate to the AirTag product page or search for AirTag on apple.com, then verify the latest AirTag model's pricing, availability, and any bulk purchase options.
result.actual_behavior: The agent spent most of the trajectory scrolling the Apple homepage, then clicked the iPhone hero's 'Learn more' link, navigated to apple.com/iphone, viewed iPhone lineup pricing, and exited without ever reaching AirTag content or checking pricing, availability, or bulk purchase options for AirTag.

evidence.claim: The task explicitly asks for pricing and availability of the latest Apple AirTag, including bulk purchase options, on Apple's website.

At step 23, the visible page is the Apple homepage iPhone hero with 'Learn more' and 'Shop iPhone' buttons. The high-detail inspection states AirTag, pricing, availability, and bulk-purchase information are not visible, and the clicked target is the iPhone 'Learn more' button.

After that click, step 25 shows the agent on https://www.apple.com/iphone/ viewing iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone Air cards with iPhone pricing. The high-detail inspection says no AirTag content is visible and AirTag pricing is contradicted because only iPhone prices are shown.

The final step is still on the iPhone page, showing iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone Air with prices and buttons. The high-detail inspection says the page is iPhone, not AirTag, and no AirTag pricing, availability, or bulk purchase options are visible when the agent exits.

A validated precedent exists for failures where the agent remains on the wrong target/entity instead of the requested one; although in a different domain, it supports using a target/result-verification rule rather than treating an unrelated page as satisfactory.

result.regression_rule: When the task asks for a specific product on a retail site, the agent must navigate to that product's page or search results for that product before checking details; following a clearly unrelated product link and exiting from that wrong product page is a wrong-target failure.
